coreClaim: Exporting saved character or persona sprites from the catalog requests one archive download instead of triggering one browser download per sprite.

inScope: Rust archive producer for saved sprite files
Hostable runtime HTTP dispatch and remote command allowlist
Shared sprite API wrapper and React catalog export handlers
outOfScope: Changing character or persona export envelopes

steps: Inspected issue #63 evidence: character and persona sprite export handlers iterated selected sprites and called downloadSpriteFile once per sprite.
Confirmed spriteApi and remote-runtime allowlist did not expose a sprite archive export command before the fix.
beforeEvidence: src/features/catalog/characters/components/CharacterSpritesTab.tsx exported sprites with a per-sprite download loop.
src/features/catalog/personas/components/sprites/PersonaSpritesTab.tsx exported sprites with the same per-sprite download loop.
src/shared/api/image-generation-api.ts had list/upload/delete/cleanup/restore sprite wrappers but no archive export wrapper.
src/shared/api/remote-runtime.ts had no sprite_export allowlist entry.

riskClaimMatrix.coreClaim: Saved sprite exports produce one archive artifact for selected/all sprites.
positiveRowsTested: Temporary local Rust proof created two character sprite files, exported one selected expression, decoded the ZIP payload, verified application/zip, verified <owner>/happy.png bytes, and verified unselected sad.webp was absent.
pnpm check passed after the final production diff.
negativeRowsTested: Should-not-match row: a saved sprite file named sad.webp was present next to happy.png but was absent from the decoded ZIP when expressions=["happy"].
Archive producer validates owner id as a safe path segment.
Archive producer canonicalizes file reads under app data directory.

groundTruthFacts.fact: Archive payload uses the existing binary download shape: base64, contentType, filename.
groundTruthFacts.proof: derived from the artifact by inspecting binary_download in src-tauri/src/commands/storage/exports.rs and the export_sprite_archive return value.

groundTruthFacts.fact: Archive entries preserve sprite filenames under a top-level owner folder and include manifest.json.
groundTruthFacts.proof: harness-proven by the temporary Rust proof that decoded Mira_Koi-sprites.zip and read Mira_Koi/happy.png.

groundTruthFacts.fact: Single-sprite download buttons still use the existing data URL download path.
groundTruthFacts.proof: derived from the artifact by inspecting unchanged onDownload handlers calling downloadSpriteFile in both sprite tabs.

userActionCopy.action: copy saved sprite files into the downloaded archive
copy exported sprite metadata into the archive manifest

edgeRowsTested: Selected expression archive includes selected file and excludes unselected file in temporary local Rust proof.
Remote-capable command path includes sprite_export in HTTP dispatch and remote command allowlist.
Both character and persona export handlers call the shared archive hook instead of looping downloads.
negativeRowsTested: No schema, dependency, version, auth, prompt, or release metadata files changed.
afterEvidence: cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml sprite_archive_exports_selected_character_sprites_as_single_zip: 1 passed; temporary proof removed from final diff.

manualBlockers.item: Visible browser confirmation of the native download prompt was not run.
manualBlockers.reason: The core behavior is covered by the archive producer proof, TypeScript compile, command registration, HTTP dispatch, and full repo check. The UI change has no visual layout change.
